my_thoughts: The normalization/redirection works: difficulty (normalizes difficulty of desire), redirects: to fundamental tool (self-remembering = only leverage), asserts: universal causal link (self-remembering generates desire and understanding). The student identified core problem (seeking leverage for desire), which shows they understand: desire = needs intensification (method). They'll likely accept the normalization/redirection. The episode is complete: aim → simplification → problem identification → normalization/redirection. Pattern complete.
accumulated_insight: Two-turn pattern: T1: Student asked aim question (seeking aim formulation)—simplification: aim = opposite of sleep/mechanicalness, binary choice. T2: Student identified core problem ('I see more clearly that what hinders progress is not wanting to escape mechanicalness sufficiently. How can one intensify the desire to escape?')—seeking leverage for desire via identifies core problem and requests method for desire. They understand: desire = needs intensification (method). The source_tracing mechanism normalizes: difficulty (normalizes difficulty of desire), redirects: to fundamental tool (self-remembering = only leverage), asserts: universal causal link (self-remembering generates desire and understanding). Pattern complete: aim question → simplification → problem identification → normalization/redirection. They've shifted: desire = via self-remembering (fundamental tool), not external method (normalization/redirection).
visible_response:
This is one of the constantly recurring questions which it is almost impossible to
answer... More understanding is necessary, and if you want a complete explanation,
self-remembering, because it is the only real answer.
If self-remembering increases,
all the rest increases.
